title: Lagenocarpus alboniger
intro: Lagenocarpus alboniger, also known as white capuchin grass, is a plant species in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, offering potential health benefits in treating conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lagenocarpus alboniger include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared for their medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Lagenocarpus alboniger is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is a type of flowering plant, specifically a type of monocot. It belongs to the order Poales, which includes numerous families of flowering plants. The family Cyperaceae, to which Lagenocarpus alboniger belongs, is a large and diverse group of plants that are commonly known as sedges. These plants are often found in wetland environments and are known for their distinctive, grass-like foliage.

It is often employed to treat different health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
